title: Use hyphen to make a list view
excerpt: when I’m typing an update on the task page and I use the hyphen key with a space, I want that to turn into a list with bullets

posts.author: TKingSH (Tami King)
posts.cookedHtml: when I’m typing an update on the task page and I use the hyphen key with a space, I want that to turn into a list with bullets

posts.author: shannon-cxmonday (Shannon Thao)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey Tami! Shannon here with the monday team. Thanks for flagging this with us. To clarify, when you’re writing a new update in the Updates Section, you’re hoping to automatically populate a bulleted list if you enter the hyphen key? Let me know if I’m understanding your request correctly.

I definitely see the value in populating a bulleted list automatically for efficiency. Are you looking for further optimization apart from utilizing the bulleted list feature showcased below for the Updates Section?

image750×254 10.3 KB

posts.author: TKingSH (Tami King)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi Shannon,

currently, I can use hyphens…but they do not become an automatic bulleted list…I have to type the hyphen every new line item.

The current bulleted list only works if you navigate and select it with your mouse

I don’t want to have to stop typing to create a list…kind of like on google docs or word - you type a hyphen, hit space bar, and it automatically indents and starts a list.
Then you can go up to the bullet button and select what type of bullet icon you want to use.

posts.author: shannon-cxmonday (Shannon Thao)
posts.cookedHtml: Got it! I appreciate the clarification, Tami. I’ll share this with the Product team!
